sections.summary: Financial institutions must establish a risk management framework for outsourcing activities to mitigate risks such as regulatory non-compliance and financial instability.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires financial institutions to implement risk management frameworks for outsourcing to ensure regulatory compliance and financial stability.

requirements.text: Financial institutions must establish a comprehensive policy on outsourcing prior to outsourcing any activity
Board of directors must approve outsourcing policies and material outsourcing arrangements
Senior management must implement and monitor outsourcing risk management procedures
Financial institutions must conduct due diligence on service providers including financial soundness and internal controls
Outsourcing contracts must include clauses for on-site inspections by the Bank and service provider
Financial institutions must have contingency plans for service provider failures
Material outsourcing requires prior authorization from the Bank

sections.source_quote: This Guideline is issued under the authority of section 50 of the Bank of Mauritius Act 2004 and section 100 of the Banking Act 2004.

sections.summary: External auditors must review and attest financial institutions' outsourcing policies and processes, and report material weaknesses to the Bank of Mauritius.
sections.dtia_summary: External auditors must review outsourcing policies and report material weaknesses to Bank of Mauritius for DTIA compliance.

requirements.text: External auditor should review and attest adequacy of policies and processes for outsourcing activities
External auditor should inform Bank of material weaknesses or irregularities affecting financial institution or operational risk

sections.source_quote: 5.1 The external auditor should review and attest the adequacy of the policies and processes put in place by financial institutions for outsourcing activities. They should immediately inform the Bank of any material weaknesses or irregularities that, in their opinion, might affect the well-being of the financial institution or have additional operational risk implications.

sections.summary: Section 6 supersedes the Guideline on Internet Banking's section 12 regarding outsourcing, but does not contain new obligations.
sections.source_quote: 6.1 In February 2001, the Bank issued to all banks a guideline entitled ‘Guideline on Internet Banking'. The guideline deals with issues relating to Internet banking and section 12 thereof deals exclusively with outsourcing of banks' Internet banking activity. This Guideline on Outsourcing by Financial Institutions covers broadly the outsourcing-related issues. As such, it supersedes section 12 of the Guideline on Internet Banking.

sections.summary: Financial institutions must seek Bank of Mauritius authorization before material outsourcing and report compliance status within 4 months.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial institutions must seek Bank authorization for material outsourcing and report compliance within 4 months for DTIA.

requirements.text: Financial institutions should seek prior authorization of the Bank before entering into material outsourcing
Financial institutions should assess existing outsourcing arrangements against this Guideline
Financial institutions should inform Bank in writing of compliance level and weaknesses within 4 months
Financial institutions should submit rectification plan and timeframe for weaknesses within 4 months
Financial institutions should inform Bank immediately of adverse developments affecting businesses

sections.source_quote: 7.1 This Guideline is applicable to all financial institutions falling under the regulatory purview of the Bank. It needs to be emphasized, as mentioned in section 4.1.3, that financial institutions should seek prior authorization of the Bank before entering into material outsourcing. 7.2 Financial institutions should conduct an assessment of all their existing outsourcing arrangements against this Guideline. Where the outsourcing is considered material, financial institutions should inform the Bank in writing as to the level of compliance with the Guideline and report weaknesses, if any. They should also submit a plan and timeframe on how such weaknesses would be rectified. This should be done within 4 months from the effective date of this Guideline. 7.3 Financial institutions should inform the Bank immediately, of any adverse development arising from any outsourcing arrangement that could significantly affect their businesses.
